texto: When the taste for physical gratifications among them has grown more rapidly than their education . . . the time will come when men are carried away and lose all self-restraint . . . . It is not necessary to do violence to such a people in order to strip them of the rights they enjoy
autor: they themselves willingly loosen their hold. . . . they neglect their chief business which is to remain their own masters.
explicacao: Citação de they themselves willingly loosen their hold. . . . they neglect their chief business which is to remain their own masters. sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “When the taste for physical gratifications among them has grown more rapidly than their education…” articula educacao, alexis-de-tocqueville, democracy-in-america-volume-2 em registro de educacao, com relevância para contextos como dia-chuvoso e democracy-in-america-volume-2.
